name: Crystagen
subtitle: EDP Tripeptide | Thymus Immune Bioregulator

molecular.halfLife: Minutes (short peptide); effects persist via epigenetic changes

overview: Crystagen is a Khavinson bioregulator tripeptide (EDP) identified in thymalin with selective action on thymus tissues. Developed at Russia's St. Petersburg Institute of Bioregulation and Gerontology, it enhances immune function by stimulating normal lymphocyte proliferation while inhibiting tumor cell growth. Research shows effectiveness in normalizing immunity in 82% of elderly patients (vs 56% control) and reducing respiratory infections in athletes by doubling HSP gene expression.
mechanism: Crystagen works through epigenetic regulation by influencing gene expression and protein synthesis in immune cells. It regulates the synthesis of heat-shock proteins, cytokines, and affects cell differentiation, proliferation, and apoptosis. The EDP peptide enhances spontaneous proliferative activity of normal lymphocytes while inhibiting proliferation of tumor cells (K-562 erythromyelosis). It activates B-cell immune system and thymic epithelial cells (VTEC2.H/S).
keyBenefits: Normalizes immune function in 82% of patients

items.name: Immune Dysfunction
items.description: Normalizes immunogram in elderly patients with impaired immunity.

items.name: Post-Infection Recovery
items.description: Restores immune function after infectious diseases.

items.name: Radiation/Chemo Recovery
items.description: Helps normalize immunity after radiation and chemotherapy exposure.

items.name: Athletic Immune Support
items.description: Increases stress resistance and reduces respiratory infections in athletes.

items.name: HSP Expression
items.description: Doubles expression of heat-shock protein gene HSPA1A.

items.name: Tumor Cell Inhibition
items.description: Inhibits proliferation of K-562 human erythromyelosis tumor cells.

deliveryMethods.overview: Available in capsule form for oral administration. Short peptides can be absorbed orally and reach target tissues. Typical protocol involves 10-20 day cycles, often repeated 2-3 times per year.
Injectable form available for direct administration.

interactions.notes: Both have immunoprotecting properties; different mechanisms complement each other.
Crystagen (EDP) is derived from thymalin; complementary immune effects.
Often combined in comprehensive anti-aging Khavinson protocols.
Related immune peptides; can be used together.

timeline.effects: Immune activation and gene expression modulation begins
Effects persist due to epigenetic changes

references.title: The Use of Thymalin for Immunocorrection and Molecular Aspects of Biological Activity
references.keyFindings: EDP (crystagen) identified in thymalin; regulates gene expression, HSP synthesis, cytokines, and cell differentiation.

references.title: Crystagen in Elderly Patients with Impaired Immunity
references.keyFindings: Crystagen normalized immunogram in 82% of patients vs 56% in control group.

references.title: Peptide Bioregulators in Athletes
references.journal: Bulletin of Experimental Biology and Medicine
references.keyFindings: Crystagen doubled HSP gene expression, normalized IL-6, and reduced respiratory infections in athletes.

references.title: EDP Peptide Effects on Lymphocyte Proliferation
references.journal: Cell Technologies in Biology and Medicine
references.keyFindings: EDP enhances normal lymphocyte proliferation while inhibiting K-562 tumor cell proliferation.

quickStats.storage: Capsules: room temperature; Injectable lyophilized: 2-8°C; Reconstituted: 2-8°C refrigerated
